input_row.title: Ensure backup data in Backup vaults is encrypted using customer-managed keys (CMK)
input_row.description: Backup vaults offer two encryption options: Microsoft-managed keys, which provide automatic encryption without user intervention, and customer-managed keys (CMK), which allow organizations to retain full control over their encryption keys for enhanced security and compliance.
input_row.rationale: Using customer-managed keys (CMKs) to encrypt Backup vaults enhances security by granting organizations complete control over their encryption keys. Impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Backup vaults. 2. Click the name of a Backup vault. 3. Under Manage, click Properties. 4. Ensure Encryption Settings is set to Using 'Customer-managed keys'. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each Backup vault. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Backup vaults: az dataprotection backup-vault list For each Backup vault, run the following command: az dataprotection backup-vault show --resource-group <resource-group> -- vault-name <backup-vault> Ensure that under properties > securitySettings > encryptionSettings > keyVaultProperties, a key keyUri exists with the value set to a customer-managed key URI. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: d6588149-9f06-462c-a076-56aece45b5ba • Name: '[Preview]: Azure Backup Vaults should use customer-managed keys for encrypting backup data. Also an option to enforce Infra Encryption.'
input_row.remediation: Note: Once encryption is configured to use a customer-managed key, this setting cannot be reversed. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Backup vaults. 2. Click the name of a Backup vault. 3. Under Manage, click Properties. 4. Under Using 'Microsoft-managed keys', click Update. 5. Check the box next to Use your own key. 6. Under Encryption key, click the radio button next to Enter key URI to provide a known key URI, or click the radio button next to Select from Key Vault to select a key from a Key Vault. 7. If entering a key URI, provide the key URI in the text box under Key URI. 8. If selecting a key from a Key Vault, click select key from Key Vault. 1. Select Key vault or Managed HSM. 2. Select a key vault or managed HSM. 3. Select a key. 4. Click Select. 9. Select a managed identity to use for the encryption key. 10. Click Update. 11. Repeat steps 1-10 for each Backup vault. Remediate from Azure CLI For each Backup vault requiring remediation, run the following command to assign a customer-managed encryption key: az dataprotection backup-vault update --resource-group <resource-group> -- vault-name <backup-vault> --cmk-encryption-key-uri <cmk-uri> --cmk- encryption-state "Enabled" --cmk-identity-type "SystemAssigned" Note: Use --cmk-identity-type "UserAssigned" --cmk-user-assigned- identity-id <user-assigned-identity-id> with the above command to provide a UserAssigned Identity Id. Default Value: By default, data in the Backup vault is encrypted using Microsoft-managed keys. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk-for- backup-vault 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/dataprotection/backup-vault 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure- resources/how-manage-user-assigned-managed-identities Additional Information: • To enable encryption, it is necessary to grant the Backup vault the appropriate permissions to access the encryption key in the key vault. The key can be modified as needed. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk-for- backup-vault#assign-permissions-to-the-backup-vault-to-access-the-encryption- key-in-azure-key-vault. • Azure Backup uses system-assigned managed identities and user-assigned managed identities to authenticate the Backup vault to access encryption keys stored in Azure Key Vault. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk-for- backup-vault#enable-a-managed-identity-for-your-backup-vault.
input_row.impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk-for- backup-vault 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/dataprotection/backup-vault 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure- resources/how-manage-user-assigned-managed-identities Additional Information: • To enable encryption, it is necessary to grant the Backup vault the appropriate permissions to access the encryption key in the key vault. The key can be modified as needed. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk-for- backup-vault#assign-permissions-to-the-backup-vault-to-access-the-encryption- key-in-azure-key-vault. • Azure Backup uses system-assigned managed identities and user-assigned managed identities to authenticate the Backup vault to access encryption keys stored in Azure Key Vault. Refer to the following guide for details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/encryption-at-rest-with-cmk-for- backup-vault#enable-a-managed-identity-for-your-backup-vault.

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_backup_vault_customer_managed_keys_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will perform the following checks:

1. **Use Azure CLI to List Backup Vaults**: 
   Command:
   ```bash
   az dataprotection backup-vault list
   ```
   This command will enumerate all Backup Vaults across the subscriptions or specific resource groups. 

2. **Check Encryption Settings for Each Backup Vault**:
   For each Backup Vault retrieved, execute:
   ```bash
   az dataprotection backup-vault show --resource-group <resource-group> --vault-name <backup-vault>
   ```
   - **Configuration Properties to Validate**: Inspect the `properties.securitySettings.encryptionSettings.keyVaultProperties` for the presence of a `keyUri` that points to a customer-managed key. Ensure `cmk-encryption-state` is set to "Enabled".

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - **Pass**: If the `keyUri` exists and is set to a customer-managed key (URI), indicating that the Backup Vault is utilizing customer-managed encryption keys.
   - **Fail**: If `keyUri` is absent or set to Microsoft-managed keys, indicating non-compliance.

4. **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command for Verification**:
   This can be combined through scripting to enable automated reporting on compliance status across all Backup Vaults, ensuring they utilize customer-managed keys for encryption.

second_opinion.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of Azure CLI commands to list and check the encryption settings of Backup Vaults is a valid automated method. Azure CLI and Azure APIs provide the necessary capabilities to perform these checks programmatically, which aligns with the automated classification.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly correct. The use of `az dataprotection backup-vault list` and `az dataprotection backup-vault show` commands are appropriate for retrieving Backup Vaults and inspecting their encryption settings.
- The program name `azure_backup_vault_customer_managed_keys_enabled` follows the convention but could be more descriptive, such as `azure_dataprotection_backup_vault_cmk_check`.
- The check for `properties.securitySettings.encryptionSettings.keyVaultProperties` and the presence of a `keyUri` is accurate. However, it should also verify that the `cmk-encryption-state` is explicitly set to "Enabled" to ensure compliance.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The assessment could be improved by including error handling and logging in the automation script to handle cases where the CLI commands fail or return unexpected results.
- Consideration for different Azure regions or subscriptions should be included to ensure comprehensive coverage.
- An additional check for permissions on the Key Vault should be included to ensure that the Backup Vault has the necessary access to the customer-managed key.
- Provide a sample script or pseudocode for better clarity on how to automate the checks.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The approach and technical details provided are well-aligned with Azure's capabilities and best practices for ensuring compliance with customer-managed key encryption in Backup Vaults.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Enhance the automation script with error handling, logging, and additional checks for Key Vault permissions. Consider renaming the program to better reflect its function and ensure clarity.
- The automated approach is appropriate, but the implementation details could be refined for robustness and completeness.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit can be automated using Azure CLI commands, which are capable of listing Backup Vaults and checking their encryption settings. The properties required to verify the use of customer-managed keys are accessible through Azure's APIs, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_dataprotection_backup_vault_cmk_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** Azure Backup Vaults
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - List Backup Vaults: `az dataprotection backup-vault list`
  - Show Backup Vault Details: `az dataprotection backup-vault show --resource-group <resource-group> --vault-name <backup-vault>`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - `properties.securitySettings.encryptionSettings.keyVaultProperties.keyUri`
  - `properties.securitySettings.encryptionSettings.cmk-encryption-state`
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - `keyUri` is present and points to a customer-managed key.
  - `cmk-encryption-state` is set to "Enabled".
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - `keyUri` is missing or does not point to a customer-managed key.
  - `cmk-encryption-state` is not "Enabled".

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name, which has been adopted.
